name: Empty Mountains Trail
name_original: Montañas Vacías – circular por El Cuervo desde Castielfabib
description: This difficult 35.7-mile touring cycling route from Castielfabib explores the remote "Empty Mountains" with 4169 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Castielfabib offers routes through a mountainous landscape in the Rincón de Ademuz region, with altitudes ranging from 800 to 1,550 meters. The area features rugged terrain and significant elevation changes, providing challenging cycling experiences. Key natural features include the Ebrón River Canyon and the Muela de los Tres Reinos Natural Park, which contribute to diverse geological formations and elevated areas. Best touring cycling routes around Castielfabib The most popular touring cycling route is Empty Mountains Trail, a 35.7 miles (57.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation changes across mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is The Staircase of Teruel – Teruel Cathedral Square loop from Ademuz, a difficult 52.8 miles (85.0 km) path. This route leads through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 5 minutes. Local touring cyclists also love the Peña Rubia – Molino Los Cuchillos loop from Ademuz, a 39.9 miles (64.2 km) trail leading through elevated regional landscapes, often completed in about 6 hours 45 minutes. Touring cycling around Castielfabib is defined by mountainous terrain, the Ebrón River Canyon, and the Muela de los Tres Reinos Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes present significant challenges. The routes in Castielfabib are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 2 reviews. More than 40 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Castielfabib's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How challenging are the touring cycling routes around Castielfabib?
A: The touring cycling routes in Castielfabib are generally quite challenging. The region is characterized by a mountainous landscape with altitudes ranging from 800 to 1,550 meters, meaning significant elevation changes are common. All 8 routes listed in this guide are rated as difficult, offering rewarding experiences for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Castielfabib?
A: You can expect varied and mountainous terrain. Routes often traverse diverse geological formations, including the dramatic Ebrón River canyon and areas within the Muela de los Tres Reinos Natural Park. The landscape features significant elevations, providing both challenging climbs and scenic descents.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Castielfabib?
A: Yes, several routes around Castielfabib are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31502847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de San Miguel – Losilla Pass loop from Ademuz&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Staircase of Teruel – Teruel Cathedral Square loop from Ademuz&lt;/a&gt; are popular options that bring you back to your starting point.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while cycling around Castielfabib?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You might encounter the spectacular geological formations of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2158736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Ebrón River&lt;/a&gt;, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2659610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge and Walkway of the Estrechos del Río Ebrón&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2158680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calicanto Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural feature. The Muela de los Tres Reinos Natural Park also offers magical landscapes and caves.

Q: Can I explore historical sites or cultural attractions on my touring bike in Castielfabib?
A: While the focus is on natural landscapes, Castielfabib itself is a historic town with a complex walled enclosure and narrow, winding streets. You can explore medieval monuments like the ruins of the castle and the Church of Nuestra Señora de los Ángeles. The broader region also features sites like the Abrigo de las Lomas de Abril, a World Heritage site known for its Levantine rock art.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Castielfabib?
A: While specific designated cyclist parking isn't detailed, Castielfabib is a small municipality. You should be able to find general parking within or near the town center, which serves as a common starting point for many routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Castielfabib?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for varied weather, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling in Castielfabib. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Castielfabib?
A: The touring cycling routes around Castielfabib are predominantly rated as difficult due to significant elevation changes and mountainous terrain. This makes them less suitable for young children or families seeking very easy, flat paths. More experienced families might find sections enjoyable, but beginners should be aware of the challenges.

Q: Are the touring cycling trails around Castielfabib dog-friendly?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are generally accessible for dogs on a leash, specific regulations for dog access on cycling trails can vary. It's always best to check local signage or contact the regional tourism office for the Muela de los Tres Reinos Natural Park or other specific areas to confirm dog-friendly policies before heading out.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay overnight for cyclists in Castielfabib?
A: Castielfabib is a rural gem, and while it's a smaller community, you can find local establishments. There are likely cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within the town itself or in nearby villages in the Rincón de Ademuz region. It's recommended to book accommodation in advance, especially during peak seasons.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Castielfabib?
A: The touring cycling routes in Castielfabib are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain and the challenging yet rewarding experiences offered by the mountainous landscape and natural features like the Ebrón River canyon.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the touring cycling routes in Castielfabib?
A: Yes, the mountainous nature of the region provides numerous opportunities for breathtaking views. The geodesic 'La Cruz de los Tres Reinos' in the Muela de los Tres Reinos Natural Park, marking the historic meeting point of three ancient kingdoms, offers panoramic vistas. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956332850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empty Mountains Trail&lt;/a&gt; also traverse elevated areas with expansive outlooks.

Q: Can I reach Castielfabib by public transport with my bike?
A: Public transport options to Castielfabib, especially those accommodating bicycles, might be limited due to its rural location. It's advisable to research specific bus routes or regional train services to the wider Valencia area and then plan for local connections, which may require a car or a taxi service for the final leg with your bike.
tips: A village with a fairy-tale appearance, which looks as if it has come from another time and another continent.
And one of the most beautiful descents of the route.

At this point was the triple border between the kingdoms of Aragon, Valencia and Castile and is currently the border between the provinces of Teruel, Valencia and Cuenca. The point is located at an altitude of 1558 m.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is quite demanding and typically takes around 5.5 hours to complete. This duration is for cycling time and does not include stops for rest, food, or enjoying the views.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult. It involves significant elevation gain and can feature rugged terrain, including gravel forest roads, doubletracks, and some paved sections. It's best suited for experienced bikepackers and cyclists with good fitness.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular loop that starts and ends in Castielfabib. As a historic village, parking might be limited directly at the trailhead, but you should find suitable parking within the village or its immediate vicinity.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a diverse rural mountain setting. The terrain includes gravel forest roads, doubletracks, and some paved sections. Expect physically demanding climbs and descents, which can sometimes have loose boulders. The landscape features extensive pine forests, high-elevation plateaus, and stunning panoramic views.

faqs.question: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several points of interest. You'll pass through the historic village of Castielfabib, encounter the Cross of the Three Kingdoms, and enjoy the First views of the Javalambre Massif. The area is also known for its dramatic landscapes and remote refugios.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for demanding conditions, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for cycling this route. The weather is typically milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially snowy conditions of winter at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in the Spanish outdoors, this is a demanding touring bicycle route. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are fit enough for long distances and varied terrain, and always keep them on a leash, especially due to potential wildlife encounters and shared paths.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Rincón de Ademuz region?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or access the general Rincón de Ademuz region. However, always respect private property and any local regulations you might encounter.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The region is known for its abundant wildlife. You might spot various raptors soaring overhead, wild cats, and red squirrels. The diverse ecosystems support unique flora and fauna, offering opportunities for wildlife observation.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route partially follows sections of other trails. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the MontañasVacías route (53%) and also includes parts of the Cruz de los tres reinos (12%) and Sendero de la Biodiversidad (5%).

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and the region's remoteness, careful planning is essential. You should bring plenty of water and food, as resupply points are rare. Low gearing is recommended for the climbs, and appropriate tires (40-42mm minimum) are advisable for the mixed terrain. Navigation tools, repair kits, and layers of clothing for changing weather are also crucial.

waypoints.name: First views of the Javalambre Massif
